mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding one percentage point signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer spending and residential investment, which directly boost corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of expansion-phase observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes during contraction phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 265.0, accelerating inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >50% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 265.0, or the threshold effect is not statistically distinguishable from a linear trend.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when payrolls rise but unemployment remains flat, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks or weakening demand, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing (t-stat < 1.5).
